description_long: This is a vintage Haking 100X film camera, characterized by its boxy, rectangular body and visibly worn, textured exterior. The front features a prominent circular lens labeled 'Color Corrected' and a rectangular viewfinder window, with the brand name 'HAKING' and model number clearly displayed. The top of the camera has a green shutter button and a winding dial, while the back includes a film advance knob and a small window for film information. The sides show signs of age, with visible screws and a latch mechanism for opening the camera to load film. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian design, primarily constructed from plastic and metal, with a matte, slightly rough finish and areas of paint wear.
description: A vintage Haking 100X film camera with a boxy, worn plastic and metal body, featuring a prominent lens, green shutter button, and winding dial.
description_view_0: Front view showing the lens, brand label, viewfinder, and textured surface with visible wear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the boxy shape, latch mechanism, and the green shutter button on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the film advance knob, film window, and winding instructions.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain side panel, screws, and the top-mounted green shutter button.
